GoalOS Loop ReactorState · trace · restart · proof
ConciergeCommand CenterLoop RoomDay-Scale LoopLoop ReactorEvidenceLegal
Long-running agent loop · evidence reactor · restart-safe

The model may wander.
The loop must prove.

Run a public-safe reactor for agents that operate beyond a single prompt: negotiate the contract first, separate roles, write state to a virtual disk, read traces, crash and restart, score subjective quality with an explicit rubric, delete obsolete harness, and expose the next bottleneck.

No account. No form. No wallet. No token approval. No network request. No cookies. No analytics. No browser storage. No transaction broadcast. No user data wanted.

Reactor core

ContractDiskPlanBuildVerifyTraceRestartDocket
0cycle
0%proof
100proof debt
68harness drag
01 · Contract first
The preset is public-safe demonstration content. The page never asks for private information.
02 · Virtual disk
contract.jsonAwaiting mission contract.
progress.mdNo loop cycle has run.
log.mdTrace not yet emitted.
bottleneck.mdInitial bottleneck: define done.
03 · Loop rail
READY · contract not yet negotiated

Define success, failure, authority, and stop conditions before the first worker acts.

  1. Reactor waiting for a bounded loop.
04 · Reactor controls
05 · Compare loop regimes
PromptOne-shot outputpersuasive but fragile
Agent loopRepeated activityuseful but ungoverned
GoalOS loopProof-gated state machinereview-ready work
06 · Current bottleneck

Done condition

The first bottleneck is not generation. It is knowing what DONE means before work starts.

Accepted proof0
Rejected traces0
Restarts0
Harness removed0
Why this is GoalOS-native

A loop becomes an institution only when each cycle emits proof.

I

Write the loop, not the prompt

The leverage is not one better instruction. It is a repeatable procedure that gathers, acts, verifies, records, and restarts.

II

Separate the roles

The planner does not grade the builder. The evaluator does not rewrite the contract. The trace reader reports divergence.

III

Negotiate the contract first

The mission contract defines acceptance tests, forbidden shortcuts, authority, risk, and the stop condition.

IV

Write state to disk

The reactor uses virtual files: contract, progress, trace, bottleneck. State is reviewable, not hidden in context.

V

Let the loop restart

A clean restart from written state is a success condition, not a failure. It proves the procedure is not context luck.

VI

Score the subjective

Taste becomes actionable only when design, originality, craft, and function are written as a rubric.

VII

Read the traces

The trace is where the loop reveals wrong assumptions, evaluator drift, and proof debt.

VIII

Delete the harness

When a scaffold stops adding value, it becomes drag. The reactor makes harness weight visible.

IX

The bottleneck moves

Planning, building, verification, taste, and communication take turns becoming the constraint. GoalOS exposes the next one.

GoalOS proof chain

Objective → contract → loop → trace → proof → Docket → Chronicle → capability.

The reactor is not a model benchmark and not an external audit. It is a public-safe demonstration of control architecture: a long-running agent loop should produce a replayable LoopDocket, not a vague story about work.

ObjectiveMission ContractBounded RolesTraceVerifierEvidence DocketGoverned Decision StateChronicleReusable Capability
